content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:打扰一下，Thompson博士。我能跟你说几句话吗?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Excuse me, Dr Thompson. May I speak to you for a minute?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:当然可以。请进。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Of course. Please come in.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:我是ALEXANDRA Jones。我在这所大学学商科，我的作业是对拥有自己公司的人进行调查。我听说你是自己公司的创始人，我想知道我是否可以问你一些关于它的问题。
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: I'm Alexandra Jones. I'm studying Business here at the university and for my assignment I have to carry out a survey of people who own their own organisations.I heard that you are the founder of your own company, and I was wondering if I could ask you some questions about it.

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:是的，当然，请说吧。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Yes, of course, please fire away.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:好的，我们开始吧!你在创办公司的过程中遇到过什么问题吗?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: OK great, we'll get started! Did you encounter any problems whilst you were starting your company?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:实际上没有我想象的那么难。我一开始就有非常明确的目标，所以我确切地知道我想要我的企业形象是什么。由于伦敦房地产市场竞争激烈，找到合适的场地非常困难，但最终我找到了一个完美的地点。最困难的部分是构建业务，因为我没有正式的模板，所以我必须自己创建。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Actually it was not as hard as I anticipated. I started out with very clear objectives, so I knew exactly what I wanted my corporate identity to be. It was very difficult to find the correct premises as the property market in London is so competitive,but eventually I found the perfect site. The hardest part was structuring the business, as I had no formal template so I had to create it myself.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:是的，这非常令人印象深刻。和员工相处困难吗?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Yes, that is very impressive. Was it difficult dealing with staff?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:要满足一群有不同兴趣的人总是很棘手的，不过，我没有遇到太多问题。我发现让员工感到被赏识是非常重要的，所以我举办了一个员工日，大家都可以自我介绍
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: It's always tricky trying to satisfy a group of people with varying interests, however, I didn't encounter many problems. I found it very important to make the staff feel appreciated, so I hosted a staff day where we could all introduce ourselves

content.cn_text: 然后互相了解。我还能够与他们谈论他们将要做的工作类型，并让他们有机会与我分享他们在工作分配方面遇到的问题。
content.en_text: and get to know each other as individuals. I was also able to talk to them about the type of work that they would be doing and gave them the opportunity to share with me the issues they had with the allocation of the work.

content.cn_text: 亚历山德拉:好的。你是雇佣了一个管理团队，还是你担任了经理的角色?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: OK. Did you hire a management team or did you take on the role of the manager?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:哦，不，我已经有很多事情要处理了。我雇佣了另一个人来担任经理的角色，然而，他在做决定之前从未征求过我的意见，所以我被迫让他离开，另雇他人。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Oh no, I already had enough tasks to take care of. I employed someone else to take on the role of the manager, however, he never consulted me before making decisions so I was forced to let him go and hire someone else.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:其他员工怎么看新经理?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: And what did the other staff think of the new manager?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:他们非常尊敬他。在雇用他之前，我与他们进行了协商，这无疑对我有所帮助。他们感到惊讶的是，尽管会议时间更长，但他在公司的角色却如此成功。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: They had a great deal of respect for him. It definitely helped that I consulted with them before hiring him. They were surprised that he has been so successful in his role at the company, despite the longer meeting hours.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:公司开业以来，有什么了不起的成就吗?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Since the company opened, has it had any great achievements?

content.cn_text: 汤姆森:我对公司的许多方面都感到满意，但有一项成就令我特别自豪。我想要一幅壁画来照亮我们的入口区域，所以我决定邀请当地的小学为我们创作一幅壁画。孩子们度过了一段美好的时光，我知道他们很高兴能加入。
content.en_text: MR THOMSON: There are many aspects of the company that I am pleased with,however, there is one achievement of which I am particularly proud. I wanted a mural painted to brighten up our entrance area, so I decided it would be a great idea to invite the local primary school to create one for us. The youngsters had a great time and I know they were thrilled to be included.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:哦，太好了。
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Oh, that's lovely.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:你开公司以来遇到过什么大问题吗?
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Have you encountered any major problems since you opened the company?

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:是的，我们总是遇到就业问题，因为很难找到适合我们提供的职位的人，但不幸的是，没有解决办法。我们的一位管理人员最近也去休产假了，这使我们陷入了一个棘手的局面，然而，我们已经设法找到了一个临时接替她的人。现在也是我们必须进行财务培训的时期，所以我需要找一个足够大的场地来容纳所有人。组织这样的活动一定很有趣!
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Yes, we are always encountering issues with employment because it is very hard to find people who are qualified for the roles that we were offering, but unfortunately there is no solution to this. One of our administrators also recently left to go on maternity leave, which put us in a tricky situation, however, we have managed to find a temporary replacement for her. It's also now approaching the period when we must carry out our financial training so I need to find a venue with enough space to hold everyone. It will be interesting trying to organise that event!

content.cn_text: 哦，天哪，我能想象。我发现自己已经很难组织起来了!
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: Oh gosh, I can imagine. I find it hard enough to organise myself!

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:组织是一件很难掌握的事情，但它也是成功的必要条件!当我学习如何保持条理时，我意识到动机的作用是至关重要的。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: Organisation is a difficult thing to master, but it is also essential for success! Whilst I was learning how to stay organised, I realised that the role of motivation is essential.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:好，太好了!
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: OK, that's great!

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:我还建议你去图书馆看看，因为那里有一个关于
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: I also suggest that you visit the library, as there is a section on

content.cn_text: 文化，其中会有一些关于如何发展你的组织技能的非常有价值的材料。
content.en_text: culture that will have some very valuable material on how to develop yourorganisation skills.

content.cn_text: ALEXANDRA:好的，我们结束后我会去图书馆。
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: OK, I'll head to the library when we're finished.

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:我想你会发现它很有用。他们还收集了一些关于个人组织的纪录片，我建议你查阅一下关于变革管理的文献。我个人认为这些资源非常有用。文章也是一种宝贵的资源，我们大学图书馆里有大量的文章。我建议你看看第2部分，在那里你可以找到关于志愿服务的性质的文章。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: I think you will find it very useful. They also have a collection of documentaries on the subject of personal organisation and I suggest that you look up the literature on management of change. I personally found these sources incredibly useful. Articles are also a valuable resource and we have a whole collection of them in our university library. I advise that you look at Section 2 where you will find articles on the  nature of volunteering.

content.cn_text: 太好了，非常感谢你的帮助。
content.en_text: ALEXANDRA: That's great, thank you so much for your help.

content.cn_text: 汤普森先生:没问题。
content.en_text: MR THOMPSON: No problem.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What was the most difficult problem when the company was founded?
items.analysis: 文中Mr. Thompson明确指出构建业务结构是"最困难的部分"（"The hardest part"），这与题目中询问的"最困难的问题"（"the most difficult problem"）直接对应。他提到没有正式的模板（"no formal template"），这与选项B "No formal structures"在意义上是一致的。因此，B选项是正确答案。

items.title: What is the staff unsatisfied with?
items.analysis: 文中提到员工有机会分享他们对工作分配的问题（"issues they had with the allocation of the work"），这表明员工对工作分配存在不满。"allocation of the work"与选项B中的"work assignment"是同义表达，都指工作分配。因此，B选项是正确答案。

items.title: What was the problem of the manager?

options.title: A
options.content: He had a lot of tasks to do.

options.title: B
options.content: He employed too many people and let them go then.

options.title: C
options.content: He always made decisions by himself.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出经理"never consulted me before making decisions"（在做决定之前从不与我商量），这直接对应了选项C "He always made decisions by himself"（他总是自己做决定）。经理不与老板商量就做决定，实际上就是在独自做决定，这种行为导致他最终被解雇。因此，C选项是正确答案，准确描述了经理的问题。

items.title: What does the staff think of the new manager of the company?

options.title: A
options.content: They are delighted to see that the meeting time is shorter.

options.title: C
options.content: They are amazed that he has done it well.

items.analysis: 原文中提到员工对新经理的表现感到惊讶（surprised），认为他很成功（successful）。"Surprised"在语境中与"amazed"同义，表达了员工的惊讶和赞叹。此外，文中还提到员工对新经理有很大的尊重（"They had a great deal of respect for him"）。这些信息都表明员工对新经理的表现印象深刻，与选项C"They are amazed that he has done it well"最为吻合。

items.title: What achievement has the organisation made already?

options.title: A
options.content: Children were involved in painting the entrance area.

options.title: C
options.content: It built a local primary school.

items.analysis: 文中Mr. Thompson提到他对公司的一个成就特别自豪，就是邀请当地小学的学生为公司入口区域创作壁画。这既美化了公司环境，又让孩子们参与其中，体现了公司的社会责任感。这个成就不仅让公司的入口变得更加美观，还让孩子们有机会展示才能并参与到社区活动中，因此被认为是一个值得骄傲的成就。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
Complete the summary below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
Major Problems of a Company
Since the company opened, there have been many problems with employment, but there is no $17310$ He needs to find a venue for financial training. It is very hard to run an organisation and the $17311$ is of great importance to staying organised. To enhance the organisation skills, there is a section on $17312$ in the library where some valuable books can be found. In addition, the library contains some useful resources, such as a collection of documentaries on personal organisation, the literature on $17313$ , and the articles on the $17314$

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解原文中提到的问题及其解决方案。文中明确指出公司在雇佣方面遇到了困难，因为很难找到合格的人选。重要的是，文中使用了"unfortunately there is no solution to this"这个表达，直接对应了题干中的"there is no [    ]"。"solution"就是填空的正确答案。这里考察了你对否定表达的理解能力，以及从整体语境中提取关键信息的能力。记住，当你听到"unfortunately"这样的词时，往往会紧跟着重要信息，在这个例子中，它引出了问题没有解决方案这一关键点。此外，题干中的"problems"和原文中的"issues"是同义词，这也是一个重要的定位线索。

这道题的关键在于理解组织管理中动机的重要性。原文中提到"the role of motivation is essential"，这直接对应了题目中所问的重要因素。"role of motivation"恰好符合题目要求的不超过三个词的答案。此外，"essential"这个词强调了动机在保持组织有序方面的关键作用，与题目中提到的"great importance"相呼应。因此，"role of motivation"是最准确和完整的答案。记住，在听力题目中，我们常常需要注意表示重要性的词语（如essential, crucial, important等），这些词往往会引导我们找到关键信息。

这道题的关键在于准确定位和理解原文中的信息。原文中提到"there is a section on culture that will have some very valuable material on how to develop your organisation skills"，这句话直接回答了题目中问到的增强组织技能的图书馆部分。"culture"是这个部分的主题，也是题目要求填写的答案。其他提到的图书馆资源，如"management of change"或"nature of volunteering"，虽然也是有价值的资料，但并不是直接与提高组织技能相关的部分。因此，正确答案是"culture"。这个答案不仅符合题目要求的词数限制（不超过三个词），而且准确反映了原文中提到的与组织技能相关的图书馆部分。

这道题的正确答案是"management of change"，因为题目要求填写图书馆中有用资源的内容。在原文中，Mr Thompson明确建议Alexandra查阅有关"management of change"的文献，并强调他个人发现这些资源非常有用。这直接对应了题目中提到的"literature on [   ]"。此外，"management of change"是一个专业术语，符合学术资源的特点。其他提到的资源如个人组织的纪录片和志愿性质的文章，虽然也是有用的资源，但不符合题目中特指的"literature"。因此，"management of change"是最符合题意和原文内容的答案。

这道题的关键在于准确定位和理解与"articles"相关的信息。在原文中，Mr Thompson提到了几种不同的资源，包括documentaries、literature和articles。对于articles，他特别指出在图书馆的Section 2可以找到关于"the nature of volunteering"的文章。这正是我们需要填写的答案。

要注意的是，虽然原文中也提到了"management of change"，但那是与"literature"相关的，而不是与"articles"相关的。因此，正确答案应该是"nature of volunteering"。

这道题考察了学生对细节的把握能力，以及区分不同信息的能力。在听力过程中，当你听到"articles"这个关键词时，要特别注意紧随其后的信息，因为那很可能就是答案所在。同时，也要注意题目要求填写不超过三个词，"nature of volunteering"正好符合这个要求。

对于听力基础较弱的同学，可以尝试记下听到的最后几个词，因为答案常常出现在句子的末尾。同时，多练习识别关键词和句子结构，提高对细节信息的敏感度，这对提升听力水平很有帮助。
